summary: Blistering blues detours, soaring Sugaree & Comes A Time, fiery encore
blurb: The first set stays tight while the second erupts into wild blues jams, highlighted by a scorching Sugaree and an inspired Comes A Time. Jerry shreds the Johnny B. Goode encore and Brent’s organ adds weight to the ballads. Strong vocals and lively drumming keep the momentum rolling.
review: On August 26 1980 at Cleveland Public Hall the Dead delivered a show that swings from disciplined first‑set work into a second‑set full of blues‑fuelled fireworks. The opening “Sugaree” rolls straight into an electric “El Paso,” and by the time “Loser” and “Althea” hit, the band is locked in. The second set flips the script with a surprise “Little Red Rooster” right after “Don’t Ease Me In,” then launches into a monster jam that weaves through “Uncle John’s Band,” “Playing In The Band,” “Drums,” and an expansive “Space.” From there the band lifts off on a luminous “Comes A Time,” glides through the “Lost Sailor” → “Saint Of Circumstance” sequence, and lands a tight‑tight “Casey Jones” before closing with Jerry’s incendiary “Johnny B. Goode” encore. Across multiple sources Garcia is described as “out of his mind” on the encore and “blistering” on Sugaree, while Brent’s organ adds the much‑needed “meat to the bones.” The drummers toss sticks high and keep the groove rolling, and Bob’s two‑guitar front line shines especially on “Little Red Rooster.” All told, it’s a high‑energy performance that rewards repeat listens.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings are clearly positive, praising standout songs (Sugaree, Comes A Time, Little Red Rooster, Johnny B. Goode) and Jerry’s playing; one is mixed but still notes strong moments. No consistent negative comments about the band's performance appear, so a solid positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Solid soundboard capture with clear instrument separation; tight first set and blistering second set are heard in full detail, making it the most complete and balanced source.

key_highlights: Tight first‑set playing that stays on the groove
Blues‑heavy second set detours, especially Little Red Rooster and extended jams
Jerry’s incendiary guitar on Sugaree and Johnny B. Goode encore
Brent’s organ adds depth to ballads like Comes A Time

band_performance.Jerry: Out of his mind on the Johnny B. Goode encore, blistering on Sugaree and delivering a standout Comes A Time, showcasing both fiery leads and lyrical phrasing.
band_performance.Bob: Two‑guitar front line praised on Little Red Rooster; vocal delivery strong and straightforward throughout.
band_performance.Brent: Organ work adds “meat to the bones” on Sugaree and provides a rich presence in the mix, especially during the ballads.
band_performance.Mickey: Lively drumming with high‑energy stick tossing during Johnny B. Goode; keeps the groove propulsive throughout the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Dynamic, lively percussion that drives the jams forward, complementing Mickey’s rhythms and enhancing the blues detours.
